-Extermely busy day today!
started off with  warmup and introduction to hermes/openclaw video about halfway through needs to be finished tomm in the AM before we headout. 
Followup with julie tomm task set for 10 am for hv portal.
[Jeff AFC Haledon owner](https://app.notion.com/p/Jeff-AFC-Haledon-owner-36ea315859b480af9fffced08daf2085?source=copy_link) using these notes today we had met with Jeff owner of an AFC, extremely well connected individual with loads of opportunites to do busines. immediate paint points is missing USCIS civil surgoen- use dr lau already left notes and actionable next steps below meeting notes. Visit more "The Pack"(his gym) classes and touch base with time to time. After getting quotes from Ryans GPO we can save them loads of money on reagents for inhouse testing on his inhouse commercial population.
Next meeting was with Yan Goldberg Owner of BeWell Article 28. The account is finally closed and we can begin by integrating within their fulton street location. Operates under a friendship basis grow closer, go out for dinners visit more often catch up most importantly make sure to find him a primary care- use abids network looking for any PCPs looking to work.- make this a task on google tasks pls. Apporeached pretty often by lincoln reps only wants to do business with people closer to hm (hes connected with yili huang Huang cardiology from lincoln lab tab) and open to maitaining good communications moving forward. Yan is ALL about relationships so a new primary goal of ours should be find him an internists at the earliest converience possible, line up multiple candidates when we can.

When it comes to rey(cardio guy)- lots of new oppoutunes have been opening actionable net steps and creation in the lincoln lab workspace, we can do several things. Start by connecting with his list of cardiologists to get referals for repatha, and other specialty medications for his patients. Prepare a blank referal guide and walk him through the process. if he lands meetings for us we can send them to our specialiy pharmacy and genrerate revenue fast with his lists of relationships. For his account Medspan Associates, FNP is from abid(get contact asap) Lisette Escobales. Get a Gyn(task for guerchon)NP or Midlevel for their practice as well. Also needs Gi (ideal time to grasp is from january and feb according to claude when they just graduate from residency).Speak with maybe pierre hindys ppl thru claude if we can find a way to grasp a new midlevel or kevin tin for their practice. 

For Nina Aronov- she is venteruing into functional medicine. Immediat eto do list is gather what exactly her team is looking to test and panel costs. Submit Marina moysieva panels to her to start followed by accu supported expansive panels with doc +excel file to get feedback. prepare prosal see what we need to do to get adjusted to thier workflow and get pricing agreements in place.

This evening a massive meeting for United Ipa happened. Disucssing the future purchasing of soverign pheonix evertyhting starts in discression. There is a $1.2 million loan, which would not be part of our 51. Ideally we would want to buy in for as little money as possible but the way it seems the current management would like to have a minimum buy-in of $250,000. The structure would go as follows:

- Doc pro for the billing and data
- Aaron and Ahmed for provider engagement and enrollment
- Luis for contract negotiation

The value proposition for this acquiring shifts significantly from health first being their most valuable asset to the Aetna Medicare shared savings contract, which is level 2 with a 50% upside and no downside risk for this year. Payouts would be in about three months. Currently they have a panel of about 750 providers and a 2.5-star rating. If we can get it to 4 stars, we get $12 per patient per month in admin fee. If we are able to leverage Lincoln's existing lab data and identify crucial Aetna Medicare providers, we can onboard them and rapidly multiply our potential for earning and even shift around the providers that are unchangeable. With this little cash being placed up front as possible in the shared savings revenue, we are able to use that cash injection that we generate to buy out more of the IPA away from its current owners. 

Purchasing parties would be split between 4 to come up with the cashflow ahmed/luis/aaron/docpro even split

Only way to purchse the IPA would be if we are able to retain the contracts and show other healthplans the turnover work we had done with using Aetna as our greatest example


For Alex Acosta, his dr Alex Rybstein is a no go on collecting cardio scripts but Localmd an existiign client just got credentialed with cardio in a few months will start sending out scipts directly to our pharmacy no issues so case still closed and still a win in our books.
